TeachLive educational program that places teachers Earthwatch expeditions, where they receive unique professional development experience teach "live" back their classrooms.
While expedition teachers interact with their students live forums, post daily highlights photos, communicate with their classrooms Skype other online tools.
This experience knowledge, skills enthusiasm teachers bring back from field gives students perspective scientific research builds their core skills science, geography other subjects.
TeachLive also unique professional development opportunity, where teachers learn about science from world-class scientists, learn research techniques they their schools.
Teachers involved past TeachLive expeditions have gone neighbourhood 'bioblitzes' with their students, undertake research into impact environmental issues local species conduct other innovative educational programs.
Bush Blitz Australia's largest nature discovery project unique multi-million dollar partnership between Australian Government through Parks Australia Australian Biological Resources Study, Billiton Sustainable Communities Earthwatch Australia document plants animals across Australia.
eighth year, largest biological survey this scale anywhere world.
Since program began 2009 Bush Blitz discovered over 1630 species animals, plants, funghi lichens.
Additionally, thousands species records have been added what already known, increasing scientific knowledge help protect Australia's biodiversity generations come.
There many plants animals still discovered science.
There estimated 580,000-680,000 species Australia, three-quarters this biodiversity identified.
Forty-five cent continental Australia over cent marine area have never been comprehensively surveyed scientists.
Bush Blitz TeachLive program placed teachers expeditions remote locations around Australia, from remote islands Coral rocky gorges Kimberley vast expanse Australian outback.
